title: Service + DTO — Recipe API
description:
### Your Task

Build a Recipe CRUD API combining **Service Layer** and **DTO Pattern** together.

### Requirements

- `Recipe` entity: `id` (Long), `title` (String), `description` (String), `cookingTime` (Integer, in minutes), `servings` (Integer), `difficulty` (String)
- `RecipeRequestDTO`: `title`, `description`, `cookingTime`, `servings`, `difficulty`
- `RecipeResponseDTO`: `id`, `title`, `description`, `cookingTime`, `servings`, `difficulty`
- `RecipeRepository` extending `JpaRepository`
- `RecipeService` — handles CRUD + DTO ↔ Entity mapping
- `RecipeController` at `/api/recipes`

### Endpoints

- `GET /api/recipes` → List of RecipeResponseDTO (200)
- `GET /api/recipes/{id}` → RecipeResponseDTO (200 or 404)
- `POST /api/recipes` → Accept RecipeRequestDTO, return RecipeResponseDTO (201)
- `PUT /api/recipes/{id}` → Accept RecipeRequestDTO, return RecipeResponseDTO (200 or 404)
- `DELETE /api/recipes/{id}` → 204 or 404

### Key Concepts

- DTO mapping happens INSIDE the service layer
- Controller accepts/returns DTOs
- Service converts DTO → Entity for saving, Entity → DTO for returning
hints: Put the mapping logic in the service, not the controller
Service methods should accept DTOs and return DTOs
Controller is thin — just delegates to service
